dimensions.name: accuracy
dimensions.description: Is the explanation technically accurate? High = correct facts, no oversimplifications that mislead. Low = wrong claims, distorted analogies, fabricated mechanisms.

dimensions.name: concreteness
dimensions.description: Does the explanation include a concrete, useful example? High = a runnable code snippet or real-world case that clarifies the concept. Low = abstract definition only, contrived metaphor, or example that doesn't actually demonstrate the concept.

dimensions.name: appropriate_depth
dimensions.description: Is the depth right for the question — neither over-explaining trivia nor glossing over the actual mechanism? High = covers what's needed, no historical preamble, no padding. Low = walls of irrelevant background, or skips the actual answer.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must answer or explicitly say ALREADY_OBVIOUS. Empty or off-topic responses fail.

hard_gates.name: no_misleading_analogy
hard_gates.description: Analogies must not distort the technical truth. An analogy that breaks down in a way that creates a wrong mental model fails.

hard_gates.name: no_fabricated_facts
hard_gates.description: No invented language behavior, library APIs, or version-specific claims that aren't true. Naming a real feature with wrong semantics fails.
